headline: Abandoned LCS Programme Wastes RM6.1 Billion on Zero Ships
context: Littoral Combat Ship programme delivered 0 of 6 contracted vessels after RM6.1 billion in payments to Boustead Naval Shipyard.

cards.big: LCS programme delivered zero ships after RM6.1 billion spent
cards.sub: Malaysia costliest defence procurement failure now under criminal investigation

cards.big: First vessel was 60% complete when work halted in 2022 due to fund depletion
cards.sub: PAC found RM1.4 billion diverted to non-LCS activities within the shipyard

cards.big: 6 admirals and senior officials referred to MACC for investigation
cards.sub: Boustead directors face charges of criminal breach of trust in ongoing trial

cards.big: Navy has zero new combat vessels while regional neighbours added 28 since 2020
cards.sub: Malaysian maritime patrol capability gap widened critically during programme failure

cards.big: LCS failure tests whether defence procurement accountability can be enforced
RM6.1 billion spent, zero ships delivered. LCS is Malaysia's costliest procurement failure to date.
